summary: Iteration 14 — Frontend-only i18n retrofit review (IT/EN toggle). Tested the language toggle, IT->EN translations on the public Landing/Login/Register pages, and the whole in-app surface after logging in as demo@ordia.app: Dashboard, Orders, New Order, Customers, Notification Center, Catalog, Setup landing + all 7 Setup subpages, plus the Onboarding welcome modal, Help & tour panel and Guided tour. Verified IT mode still renders correctly, language persistence in localStorage across reloads, and that switching via the header toggle updates the whole UI live. Also checked for raw i18n keys leaking on screen (dash.totals, orders.range, erp.testOk, tour.step, review.banner, etc.) — none found. Two blocking issues discovered and one was self-fixed (Catalog `t is not a function` crash). One MAJOR miss remains: /app/setup/bridge is 100% Italian in EN mode — BridgeSetup.js does not use useI18n at all.

ui_bugs.issue: Entire page renders in Italian regardless of language setting. File does not import or call useI18n(). Every heading, description, progress-panel label, agent card, diary and readiness step is a hardcoded Italian string. Includes 30+ untranslated strings such as 'Gli ordini approvati appaiono direttamente nel tuo gestionale...', 'Il tuo Bridge questa settimana', 'bozze di prova corrette', 'codici in anagrafica', 'auto-riparazioni', 'Formato del tuo gestionale', 'Agenti Bridge', 'Nuovo agente', 'GESTIONALE / ERP', 'PROFILO DI EXPORT', 'Apprendimento del gestionale', 'Formato del gestionale appreso', 'Anagrafica clienti sincronizzata', 'Ordini di prova riusciti', 'Periodo di osservazione', 'ERP appresi (self-learning)', 'Conferma e attiva', 'Anteprima procedura appresa', 'Consegne recenti', 'Modalità apprendimento', 'Ultimo contatto', etc.

FIXED by test agent — component imported useI18n but never called it, so t() was undefined and the page crashed with the exact 't is not a function' TypeError the review request warned about (React error boundary triggered, red runtime error overlay in dev). Added `const { t } = useI18n();` at the top of Catalog(). Page now renders in both IT and EN.

FIXED by test agent — the progress-bar step chips under 'SETUP PROGRESS' rendered `s.label` raw (server returns Italian labels like 'Dati azienda', 'Catalogo prodotti', 'Apprendimento', 'Automazioni'), so in EN mode the chips showed Italian while the cards below showed English. Wrapped with `t(s.label)` so both languages resolve via the EN phrase map. Verified: labels now flip to 'Company details / Product catalog / WhatsApp Business / Email / ERP export / Team' when EN is selected.
Email input placeholder is hardcoded Italian ('tu@azienda.com') even in EN mode. Minor cosmetic leak.

The <video> element's Italian poster image shows 'Come funziona Ordia' / '90 secondi!' even in EN mode. Not translatable text (it's a rasterized poster/thumbnail) and the video content itself is IT-only. Nice-to-have: swap poster + provide an EN caption.

action_items: MAIN AGENT PRIORITY 1: Fully internationalize /app/frontend/src/pages/setup/BridgeSetup.js. Currently it has no useI18n import — the entire page is hardcoded Italian. Wrap every user-visible string (heading, subheadings, KPI captions, section titles 1-4, agent card labels, readiness bullets, learned-ERP labels, delivery labels, toasts, buttons like 'Nuovo agente', 'Conferma e attiva', 'Anteprima procedura appresa', 'Modalità apprendimento') with t(...) and add the missing entries in the EN phrase map in /app/frontend/src/context/I18nContext.js. This is the only surface where an EN user sees Italian.
MAIN AGENT PRIORITY 2: Translate the Login email input placeholder ('tu@azienda.com') via t() so EN users see e.g. 'you@company.com'.
OPTIONAL: Provide an English poster / thumbnail for the landing 90s video (currently the JPG frame reads 'Come funziona Ordia · 90 secondi!' in EN mode).
critical_code_review_comments: Recurring pattern: multiple components imported useI18n but forgot to destructure `const { t } = useI18n();` at the top of the component, leading to `t is not a function` crashes (Catalog was the latest instance — I fixed it; the previous fixes in OnboardingProvider, VideoPlayer, GuidedTour, SortableRow, StatusBadge were the same class of bug). Consider adding an ESLint rule or a small dev-mode wrapper that throws a clearer error when t() is called outside I18nProvider, so this pattern is caught at import time.

The EN phrase map in I18nContext.js is a single 500+ line object keyed by the Italian source string. Duplicate keys already exist ('Creazione…' and 'Crea spazio di lavoro' each appear twice). Consider deduping and splitting per feature so we don't lose translations to accidental overrides.

Setup.js renders `s.label` from the server-side steps payload as a translation key. That coupling is fragile — the moment the backend renames a step label the EN mapping silently breaks. Prefer sending stable keys from the backend and translating on the client.

BridgeSetup.js is ~500 lines with sub-components (AgentCard, ReadinessPanel, BridgeDiary, ProcedurePreview) that each have their own hardcoded IT copy. When retrofitting i18n, don't only add useI18n at the top of BridgeSetup() — every sub-component needs its own useI18n call.

Language toggle only lives in AppShell (logged-in surface) and Landing header. Login and Register do NOT show a language toggle. First-time EN visitors who navigate directly to /login must open devtools + set localStorage to force EN. Consider surfacing the toggle on the auth pages too.

success_rate.frontend: ~85% — all core pages translate correctly in both directions after fixes; only BridgeSetup and 2 minor placeholders remain untranslated in EN mode.

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 13's backend Bridge lifecycle suite is unrelated to this iteration. For i18n regression, force EN before login with `localStorage.setItem('ordia.lang','en')`. Routes are all under /app/* (NOT /dashboard, /orders etc — those redirect to /). Login post-redirect goes to /app. After next iteration, revisit /app/setup/bridge specifically to verify BridgeSetup was translated (currently 100% Italian). Also verify no raw keys leak (dash.*, orders.range, erp.testOk, tour.step, review.banner, cust.reorderAlert, etc.) — none were leaking in iter 14.
rca of the issue: 1) Catalog crash — Catalog.js had `import { useI18n } from '@/context/I18nContext'` but the function body never destructured `t` from a `useI18n()` call, so every JSX use of `t('...')` referenced an undefined identifier, throwing `TypeError: t is not a function` on mount. React error boundary caught it and returned an empty page (only 'Made with Emergent' badge). Fix: add `const { t } = useI18n();` as the first line inside Catalog(). 2) Bridge page untranslated — BridgeSetup.js was never touched during the i18n retrofit; it imports zero i18n primitives. Needs a proper translation pass across the file and its 4 sub-components, plus new entries in the EN phrase map. 3) Setup landing progress step chips — labels came directly from an API payload and were rendered raw; wrapping with t() picks up the existing EN mapping so no backend change is needed.
